title: Energizing French Hazelnuts Cassoulet
description: A buttery, chewy, hangover-cure French (Alsace) cassoulet featuring toasted hazelnuts and a paleo-friendly blend of chicken, veggies, and herbs, all cooked in a tava-griddle.
instructions: Preheat a heavy tava-griddle over medium-high heat and lightly oil the surface with olive oil.
While the griddle heats, toast the hazelnuts in a dry pan until fragrant, then set aside.
Season the chicken thighs with salt and pepper, then sear them on the griddle until golden brown on both sides.
Remove the chicken, set aside, and add the diced onion, carrot, and celery to the griddle, sautéing until softened.
Stir in the butter and let it melt, coating the vegetables.
Return the chicken to the griddle, nestle it among the vegetables, and add the chopped kale, thyme, and rosemary.
Drizzle the remaining olive oil over the mixture, then pour in enough water to cover the bottom about 1 inch.
Bring the liquid to a gentle simmer, cover, and let cook for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the toasted hazelnuts, then reduce heat and cook uncovered for an additional 10 minutes until the liquid has thickened slightly.
Taste and adjust seasoning with additional salt and pepper if needed.
Let the cassoulet rest for 5 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.
Serve hot, optionally garnishing with a sprinkle of fresh parsley or a squeeze of lemon.
